title: Why not a prequel
wikitext:
[]: Why not a prequel?             Quote Post by shinesman_cobalt » Thu Jul 19, 2007 11:27 pm I don't know if this belongs in this section or not, but I thought I would give it a shot. Recently I was thinking about the old rumors I had heard about the Rebuild series. One of which was that the four movies would consist of a prequal to the series, two movies that cover the series, and a final new ending. I thought that was a novel idea, and I espically liked the idea of a prequel. But now it seems that the prequel movie wasn't true, and the movies will be three movies covering the series, and a new ending. My question is, why not a prequel? I believe the series has told it's story. We have the TV series (plus the various "director's cuts" of it), the manga AND Death&Rebirth, all of which tell the "main" story. However the time BEFORE the third Angel and attack has BARELY been touched upon in any of these. We see a quick glimps of it in episode 21, as well as a few scattered flashbacks in the movies, but never anything substantial. There is a lot of story there that I feel is going to waste. I am curious as to what the characters lives were like before 2nd Impact. Plus there is the initial discovery of the angels, what exactly happend at 2I, the wars and conflicts that followed 2I, the begining of NERV, the UN and the Eva series, the creation of Rei, Shinji and the other pilot's births, Yui's "disappearance", Shinji and the other pilot's childhoods, and a whole bunch of other stuff that could easily fill a movie. Of course there would be no "robot fighting", but I think Eva fans would come to accept that. Why rehash an already perfect story when there is a story that hasnt been told yet? What does everyone else think about a prequel movie? Lots of unused potential, or a bad idea?

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu Jul 19, 2007 11:35 pm I say the past has a lot of unused potential, but that will probably be left to the realm of fanfiction. Myself, I would like more information on the pre-second impact world, but I would like to see it mixed in with the movie, preferably mixed in with the perfect scene to tell the story. Perhaps as Misato trying to help Shinji by telling her story, etc. Now that would be interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Jul 19, 2007 11:43 pm Why not a prequel? Prequels suck ass. They are huge pieces of shit. Every time I see a prequel I see a copout on the part of the creators.* *Please note, I just watched Twin Peaks: Fire Walk With Me , and am extremely pissed off about the entire concept of prequels and leaving plot lines unresolved.

[]:             Quote Post by shinesman_cobalt » Thu Jul 19, 2007 11:47 pm BrikHaus wrote: Why not a prequel? Prequels suck ass. They are huge pieces of shit. Every time I see a prequel I see a copout on the part of the creators.* *Please note, I just watched Twin Peaks: Fire Walk With Me , and am extremely pissed off about the entire concept of prequels and leaving plot lines unresolved.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jul 20, 2007 12:01 am If there were to be a prequel, I would want it to be that of the series/movie continuity. If there was a prequel movie with Rebuild, it would be in the continuity of Rebuild, and not of the series. Why didn't they want to make 4 movies that were prequels to the TV series? It probably would have been retcon hell.

[]:             Quote Post by shinesman_cobalt » Fri Jul 20, 2007 12:09 am Well, not 4 prequel movies. I was thinking one prequel, two "series" movies and one end movie. 4 prequels would become a little convoluted, I agree. But I just feel there is a lot of story they could use.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jul 20, 2007 12:26 am I don't think 4 prequel movies woud have been awkward at all. I guess more what I mean is like what's been done countless times with other series, having a series that occurs before the first series chronologically. As opposed to a movie that simply explains a few things leading up to the "guts" of the show, i.e. the main series. An example: create a new main character, around the same age as Misato/Ritsuko. Since the rest of the Eva cast really doesn't meet (like Yui and, say, Misato), this character is used to tie together the rest of the Eva cast. This would explain all the stuff that happened in the past, 2I, etc. while still standing by itself as a story. The end of the movie, this new character dies.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jul 20, 2007 12:45 am BrikHaus wrote: *Please note, I just watched Twin Peaks: Fire Walk With Me , and am extremely pissed off about the entire concept of prequels and leaving plot lines unresolved.

[]:             Quote Post by shinesman_cobalt » Fri Jul 20, 2007 1:48 am is the First Ancestrial Race even canon? Sounds very EU to me. XD

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Fri Jul 20, 2007 1:55 am The FAR is a bunch of ASDFDSFARGEGFGSFDSGRAHHHHHHH*breaks stuff* information that they for some reason didn't include in the series, though it was included in the original sales pitch.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jul 20, 2007 3:01 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: their Battle of the Seeds

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Jul 20, 2007 3:39 am I've always found prequels to be uninteresting. Sure they're usually lumped in with sequels in unplanned stories but prequels usually fare worse than sequel. If a movie is a success and they make a sequel than it's mostly justified where the story goes because it's a continuation. You're telling a NEW story. With a prequel you're not doing that. Prequels work more as a way to cash in and tell HOW THE FIRST STORY CAME TO BE. An actual plot comes second. I hate Star Wars overall but would the prequels had been better if they had cared less about setting up the original trilogy and more about telling their own story? I think so. And with a prequel they just open themselves up for fan scrutiny (now Rebuild is special cause it's supposed to be a non-canon remake) where something that happens in Prequel A refutes a throwaway line of dialogue in the original. I can't really see what would be the purpose of an Eva Prequel? It'd be so easy to fall into so many traps (Oh my god! Eva isn't the story of Shinji Ikari! It's actually the story of Kozu Fuyutsuki!!!!) and pointless jerk off the fans moments- look, Yui likes to wear purple and green underwear to bed. Wink wink nudge nudge. I love how the backstory in Original Eva (or shoudl it be Classic Eva? Like Classic Coke...but then again that would make R'Eva be like that new Coke in the 80s-too young to actually drink any-and that brings up bad conotations. AH! I digress!) was covered. We were let in on only the surface details and not forced to slog through all this other set up crap. Besides, had Movie 1 been an Eva prequel focusing on Gendo and the like forming NERV than wouldn't that dilute the power of the orginal characters? Heh, I'm at work and ranting. I'm glad Film 1 isn't a prequel. I like Lorentz and the rest of the Rainbow Squad (the Colorful characters at the SEELE Conference) as vague archetypes more than fully fleshed out characters.

[]: Re: Why not a prequel?             Quote Post by slothen » Fri Jul 20, 2007 4:09 am shinesman_cobalt wrote: My question is, why not a prequel? stuff What does everyone else think about a prequel movie? Lots of unused potential, or a bad idea?

[]:             Quote Post by shinesman_cobalt » Fri Jul 20, 2007 4:18 am @ gendopapa I'm tempted to throw away everythign you just said because of your "I hate star wars" line. No human being would say such a thing! But anyway, I think your focusing too much on the bad and not the good that could come of it. Will fans nitpick a prequel? Of course. Fans ALREADy nitpick the seriesbased on minoir continutity errors and throwaway lines. That wont change with any type of movie. If a prequel is a way to "cash in" on the story, then surely Rebuild, which is THE SAME STORY THAT ALREADY BEEN TOLD is cashing in on it as well. Atleast with Prequels, you get a new perspective and get to flesh out the characters. All in all, the point is to enrich the original story, not to take away from it. Everything else you said would just be the result of bad writing. I believe a prequel movie could be done correctly. Sure SOME fans will try to point out the Yui is left-handed in the series, but right handed in the movie, or some other minor detail, but like I said that happens anyway with Eva fans. @slothen Ditto to the above, but also; What is everyone problem with prequels? Has Lucas tainted the word THAT much? Anyway, I know that Rebuild is being made just as a cashcow. Otherwise, they would have given us something NEW. I was jsut saying hypothetically, a prequel could work, as it is mostly already established EVA fans going to see these movies.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Fri Jul 20, 2007 5:31 am shinesman_cobalt wrote: I was jsut saying hypothetically, a prequel could work, as it is mostly already established EVA fans going to see these movies.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jul 20, 2007 5:34 am You know a prequel could be about the origin of the Seeds and how they set everything in the series in motion.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Fri Jul 20, 2007 5:41 am sorry, but i would find that supremely uninteresting.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jul 20, 2007 6:19 am Not everyone agrees with you on that. I think expanding the canon in such a way would've been great. The plot is what hooked me on the show in the first place after all, so having the vague nonsense explained would be awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jul 20, 2007 6:34 am It's all in the way it's written and presented. If it's just 90 minutes of "this is what happened and this is why" without a story then that would be pretty boring. A great example of a prequel are the Rozen Maiden OVAs, short 2 epiodes that explains the history of the 2 main Rozen dolls from the first season, and that was made of pure win and uncut cocaine.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Thu Jul 26, 2007 2:26 pm BrikHaus wrote: *Please note, I just watched Twin Peaks: Fire Walk With Me , and am extremely pissed off about the entire concept of prequels and leaving plot lines unresolved.

20, 2007 3:39 am I've always found prequels to be uninteresting. Sure they're usually lumped in with sequels in unplanned stories but prequels usually fare worse than sequel. If a movie is a success and they make a sequel than it's mostly justified where the story goes because it's a continuation. You're telling a NEW story. With a prequel you're not doing that. Prequels work more as a way to cash in and tell HOW THE FIRST STORY CAME TO BE. An actual plot comes second. I hate Star Wars overall but would the prequels had been better if they had cared less about setting up the original trilogy and more about telling their own story? I think so. And with a prequel they just open themselves up for fan scrutiny (now Rebuild is special cause it's supposed to be a non-canon remake) where something that happens in Prequel A refutes a throwaway line of dialogue in the original. I can't really see what would be the purpose of an Eva Prequel? It'd be so easy to fall into so many traps (Oh my god! Eva isn't the story of Shinji Ikari! It's actually the story of Kozu Fuyutsuki!!!!) and pointless jerk off the fans moments- look, Yui likes to wear purple and green underwear to bed. Wink wink nudge nudge. I love how the backstory in Original Eva (or shoudl it be Classic Eva? Like Classic Coke...but then again that would make R'Eva be like that new Coke in the 80s-too young to actually drink any-and that brings up bad conotations. AH! I digress!) was covered. We were let in on only the surface details and not forced to slog through all this other set up crap. Besides, had Movie 1 been an Eva prequel focusing on Gendo and the like forming NERV than wouldn't that dilute the power of the orginal characters? Heh, I'm at work and ranting. I'm glad Film 1 isn't a prequel. I like Lorentz and the rest of the Rainbow Squad (the Colorful characters at the SEELE Conference) as vague archetypes more than fully fleshed out characters.
